project_title: 50-course Georgetown SSP registry; 7,500+ technique lines; capstone plug tags [SR/EP/IR/SC/G]
product_name: 50-course Georgetown SSP registry; 7,500+ technique lines; capstone plug tags [SR/EP/IR/SC/G]
runtime_target: packet registry, routing table, and promotion decision board
operational_end_state: A reviewer can see source identity, authority, route, output impact, and next build decision.
minimum_operational_release: A reviewable research packet for 50-course Georgetown SSP registry; 7,500+ technique lines; capstone plug tags [SR/EP/IR/SC/G]: research question, design, evidence map, analysis plan, limitations, source-use table, and next empirical or publication step.
full_build_definition: A research packet with question, method, evidence, source utilization, analysis plan, limitations, artifact outputs, review gates, and submission or conference-readiness criteria.
source_actions_before_claim: Complete 4 close-read contract(s), update 12 linked proof-capsule field(s), and preserve the current claim ceiling until passage notes exist.
claim_boundary: This is a full-build plan, not a claim that the full operational system has already been completed.
public_claim_now: May claim a full-build candidate exists with proof and source contracts; do not claim deployment, outside validation, or production readiness until live execution and passage verification are recorded.
current_status: source-backed proof build; needs source-to-output capture and close-read strengthening
first_full_build: Add promotion-router examples showing how a packet becomes a room, paper, software instrument, source reservoir, archive item, or blocked record.
launch_or_review_path: Open the project contract for `mb-georgetown-technique-registry-master-50-bundle`, assemble the research packet, and run source, method, and claim-boundary checks.

instruments.purpose: Turn the `item registry` module into a concrete input-operation-output step for 50-course Georgetown SSP registry; 7,500+ technique lines; capstone plug tags [SR/EP/IR/SC/G].

instruments.output_contract: A reviewer-visible state record, output artifact, or decision boundary tied to the project acceptance tests.
instruments.acceptance_condition: The item registry instrument is present, named, connected to the fixture, and has an output or decision field that can be reviewed.

acceptance_tests: claim ceiling test: public claims cannot exceed built evidence
source test: metadata-routed sources require close reading before citation or quotation
privacy test: no local paths or private directories in public deploy
promotion test: item cannot advance without identity, authority, route, and next action
